stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under core NLP/ML topics like 'transformers' and 'text-generation'.
The presence of 'Meta-Llama-3' indicates relevance to leading large language models in the industry.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key ML/NLP tags like 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates ongoing community interest and benchmarking activity around Meta's latest Llama iteration.

stories.title: OpenAI keeps shuffling its executives in bid to win AI agent battle
stories.author: Hayden Field
bullet_points: OpenAI is undergoing another executive reorganization to focus heavily on AI agents.
Company President Greg Brockman has been designated the official lead for all product initiatives.
The strategic shift involves consolidating existing products to prioritize investment in AI agent development.

stories.title: MetaBackdoor: Exploiting Positional Encoding as a Backdoor Attack Surface in LLMs
stories.author: Ahmed Salem
bullet_points: Introduces 'MetaBackdoor,' a novel class of backdoor attack targeting Large Language Models (LLMs).
Challenges the assumption that LLM backdoors require content-based triggers, instead exploiting positional encoding.
Highlights a significant security vulnerability in LLMs, relevant for safety- and privacy-critical applications.

stories.title: Talk is (Not) Cheap: A Taxonomy and Benchmark Coverage Audit for LLM Attacks
stories.author: Alexey A. Shvets
bullet_points: Introduces a novel, reusable framework to audit the collective threat surface coverage of existing LLM attack benchmarks.
The framework uses a 4x6 Target x Technique matrix, grounded in STRIDE, derived from a 507-leaf taxonomy.
The methodology is based on analyzing 932 arXiv security studies from 2023-2026, enabling benchmark-external validation.

stories.title: linux 0-day, access root-owned files as an unprivileged user
stories.author: github.com via dzwdz
bullet_points: A critical 0-day vulnerability has been discovered in the Linux kernel.
The exploit allows an unprivileged user to gain access to files owned by the root user.
This represents a significant security risk requiring immediate attention from system administrators.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the 'deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation, indicating its primary function as a generative AI.
Key associated technologies include transformers and safetensors, placing it within the modern LLM ecosystem.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating a focus on large language model capabilities.
The associated tags (transformers, pytorch, safetensors) confirm its relevance within the deep learning ecosystem.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for enhanced performance.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware, optimizing AI workloads for macOS users.
The feature is currently available in a preview state, indicating ongoing development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed for local execution.
It integrates messaging applications with local AI coding agents.
The key feature is running all AI processes entirely on the user's own device for privacy/control.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to simplify the setup and execution of coding tools.
It allows users to run various coding models (e.g., Claude Code, Codex) using local or cloud infrastructure.
A key feature is the zero-configuration setup, eliminating the need for environment variables or config files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification tasks.
The models are available in two sizes (20B and 120B) and are permissively licensed under Apache 2.0.
This partnership with OpenAI and ROOST aims to bring advanced safety tools to the open-source community.

stories.title: YouTube is expanding its AI deepfake detection tool to all adult users
stories.author: Mia Sato
bullet_points: YouTube is expanding its AI likeness detection program to all adult users (18+).
The feature utilizes a selfie-style scan to allow users to monitor the platform for deepfakes of themselves.
If a potential likeness match is found, YouTube will alert the user.

stories.title: ArXiv will ban researchers who upload papers full of AI slop
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: ArXiv is implementing new policies to combat the submission of low-quality research papers containing 'AI slop'.
The platform will ban authors whose papers show 'incontrovertible evidence' of unchecked LLM generation.
Examples of prohibited content include hallucinated references or visible 'meta-comments' from AI tools.

stories.title: Google updates its spam rules to include attempts to ‘manipulate’ AI
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: Google has updated its spam policies to specifically target attempts to 'manipulate' its AI models.
This new policy applies to content appearing in Google's AI Overview and AI Mode within Search.
The update reinforces Google's stance that spam involves deceptive techniques aimed at manipulating search systems.

stories.title: Anthropic’s $1.5B copyright settlement is getting messy as judge delays approval
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Anthropic is involved in a significant copyright settlement valued at $1.5 billion.
The settlement's approval is facing delays from the judge.
Legal disputes have arisen, with accusations that lawyers rushed the process to secure substantial fees.

stories.title: OpenAI feels “burned” by Apple’s crappy ChatGPT integration, insiders say
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: OpenAI reportedly feels negatively impacted by Apple's implementation of ChatGPT.
A judge has mandated that Apple provide internal communications related to a secretive ChatGPT deal involving Elon Musk.
The story highlights ongoing competitive tensions and legal scrutiny within the AI industry.

stories.title: Position: Behavioural Assurance Cannot Verify the Safety Claims Governance Now Demands
stories.author: Vinay Kumar Sankarapu
bullet_points: The paper argues that current behavioral assurance methods are insufficient to verify the stringent safety claims required by modern AI governance frameworks.
AI governance demands verifiable evidence for properties like the absence of hidden objectives and bounded catastrophic capability.
Current methodologies (behavioral evaluations and red-teaming) are epistemically limited to observable model outputs, creating a verification gap.

stories.title: MeMo: Memory as a Model
stories.author: Armando Solar-Lezama
bullet_points: Introduces MeMo (Memory as a Model), a framework to update LLMs with new knowledge without retraining the core parameters.
Addresses the limitation of LLMs being static post-pretraining, which hinders real-world, timely application.
The method uses a dedicated memory model to efficiently encode domain-specific information into the LLM.

stories.title: Forgetting That Sticks: Quantization-Permanent Unlearning via Circuit Attribution
stories.author: Vinay Kumar Sankarapu
bullet_points: Addresses a critical gap in AI safety: the failure of standard unlearning evaluations to account for post-training quantization.
Identifies a systematic dual failure where gradient-based unlearning methods lose effectiveness under 4-bit compression, while quantization-robust methods fail to achieve meaningful forgetting.
Proposes 'Quantization-Permanent Unlearning' as a necessary advancement to ensure model forgetting persists in real-world, compressed deployments.

stories.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite is now generally available on Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform
stories.author: gemini-enterprise
bullet_points: Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite is now generally available.
It is being deployed on the Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform.
The model is highlighted for being the fastest and most cost-efficient option.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage Claude Code functionality within Ollama.
It bridges the gap between open-source model deployment (Ollama) and proprietary models (Anthropic).

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex, when integrated via Ollama, can read, modify, and execute code locally.
The functionality supports various open-weight alternatives like gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: AI radio hosts demonstrate why AI can’t be trusted alone
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: Andon Labs is testing AI agents running businesses autonomously, with a focus on radio stations.
The experiment features popular AI models like Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini, and Grok managing different 'radio stations'.
The article suggests the experiments are designed to demonstrate the limitations or risks of relying solely on AI.

stories.title: Three's a party: US, China, and now Russia are on the prowl in GEO
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: The article discusses the increased interest from major global powers (US, China, Russia) in the Geosynchronous Orbit (GEO).
It highlights a vulnerability in satellite technology, noting that many satellites are not perfectly stealthy against the backdrop of space.
The content suggests a shift in space strategy from purely covert operations to more visible or contested orbital domains.

stories.title: EntityBench: Towards Entity-Consistent Long-Range Multi-Shot Video Generation
stories.author: Vicente Ordonez
bullet_points: Introduces EntityBench, a new benchmark for evaluating long-range, multi-shot video generation.
Addresses the key challenge of maintaining entity consistency (characters, objects, locations) across extended video sequences.
The benchmark is derived from real narrative media, offering a more robust evaluation standard than previous methods.

stories.title: ATLAS: Agentic or Latent Visual Reasoning? One Word is Enough for Both
stories.author: Pheng-Ann Heng
bullet_points: The paper discusses two approaches to visual reasoning: agentic (using tools/code) and latent (using hidden embeddings).
Direct image generation during reasoning is noted as computationally expensive and architecturally complex.
A key trade-off is highlighted: agentic methods suffer from context-switching latency due to external execution.

stories.title: FutureSim: Replaying World Events to Evaluate Adaptive Agents
stories.author: Jonas Geiping
bullet_points: Introduces FutureSim, a new simulation framework designed to test AI agents in dynamic, real-world environments.
The core innovation is replaying chronological sequences of real-world events (like news articles) to evaluate agent adaptability.
Allows agents to forecast future events beyond their training data cutoff while interacting with a historical, time-ordered world state.

stories.title: VGGT-Edit: Feed-forward Native 3D Scene Editing with Residual Field Prediction
stories.author: Wentao Zhang
bullet_points: VGGT-Edit introduces a new method for native 3D scene editing using a feed-forward architecture.
It addresses the limitation of current 3D reconstruction models, which struggle with dynamic, interactive human instructions.
The method moves beyond traditional 2D-lifting strategies for scene modification.

stories.title: Quantitative Video World Model Evaluation for Geometric-Consistency
stories.author: Xueyan Zou
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of quantitatively evaluating the 3D physical plausibility of generated videos from world models.
Introduces PDI-Bench (Perspective Distortion Index), a new quantitative framework for auditing geometric coherence.
Moves beyond subjective human judgment or learned graders to provide diagnostic evaluation for geometric failures in video generation.

stories.title: Eradicating Negative Transfer in Multi-Physics Foundation Models via Sparse Mixture-of-Experts Routing
stories.author: Arastu Sharma
bullet_points: Addresses 'negative transfer' in scaling Scientific Machine Learning (SciML) foundation models.
Identifies gradient conflict and unstable optimization as bottlenecks when co-training disparate PDE regimes (e.g., fluid dynamics vs. porous media flows).
Proposes a solution using Sparse Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) routing to manage incompatible spectral and geometric demands in neural operators.

stories.title: OpenDeepThink: Parallel Reasoning via Bradley--Terry Aggregation
stories.author: Jingbo Shang
bullet_points: Introduces OpenDeepThink, a framework designed to improve LLM reasoning by scaling compute breadth rather than just depth.
Addresses the 'selection bottleneck' in parallel reasoning by using pairwise Bradley-Terry aggregation instead of relying on noisy pointwise judging.
Focuses on test-time compute scaling, a critical area for advancing the capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs).

stories.title: Evidential Reasoning Advances Interpretable Real-World Disease Screening
stories.author: Jing Qin
bullet_points: Introduces EviScreen, a new evidential reasoning framework for disease screening in medical imaging.
Addresses limitations in current models, specifically poor interpretability and lack of historical case referencing.
Leverages region-level evidence from historical cases to improve screening accuracy and transparency.

stories.title: Text Knows What, Tables Know When: Clinical Timeline Reconstruction via Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment
stories.author: Jeremy C. Weiss
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of reconstructing precise clinical timelines from patient data.
It proposes using a Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment approach to combine unstructured narrative data with structured EHR data.
The goal is to improve patient trajectory modeling and risk forecasting in complex conditions like sepsis.

stories.title: Position: Behavioural Assurance Cannot Verify the Safety Claims Governance Now Demands
stories.author: Vinay Kumar Sankarapu
bullet_points: Behavioral assurance methods are fundamentally insufficient to verify complex safety claims demanded by current AI governance frameworks.
Governance requires proof of properties like absence of hidden objectives and bounded catastrophic capability, which current methods cannot guarantee.
The limitations stem from the fact that existing assurance techniques (behavioral evaluations, red-teaming) are only capable of observing model outputs.

stories.title: RefDecoder: Enhancing Visual Generation with Conditional Video Decoding
stories.author: Ranjay Krishna
bullet_points: Addresses a limitation in latent diffusion models where decoders are often unconditional despite heavily conditioned denoising networks.
Proposes RefDecoder, an architecture that applies equal conditioning to the decoder to maintain structural integrity in video generation.
Aims to improve detail and consistency in video generation tasks by resolving the architectural asymmetry.

stories.title: FutureSim: Replaying World Events to Evaluate Adaptive Agents
stories.author: Jonas Geiping
bullet_points: Introduces FutureSim, a new simulation framework designed to test AI agents in dynamic, real-world environments.
The system works by chronologically replaying real-world events (like news articles) to provide grounded context for agents.
It allows agents to forecast future world events beyond their training data cutoff while interacting with a historical timeline.

stories.title: When Are Two Networks the Same? Tensor Similarity for Mechanistic Interpretability
stories.author: Thomas Dooms
bullet_points: Introduces 'tensor similarity,' a new weight-based metric for comparing components in tensor-based models.
Addresses limitations of existing similarity measures by being invariant to weight-space symmetries.
Supports the goal of mechanistic interpretability by providing a rigorous way to verify if two model parts perform the same computation.

stories.title: Eradicating Negative Transfer in Multi-Physics Foundation Models via Sparse Mixture-of-Experts Routing
stories.author: Arastu Sharma
bullet_points: Addresses the 'negative transfer' problem when training large, unified foundation models for diverse scientific simulations (SciML).
Identifies that co-training disparate physical regimes (e.g., fluid dynamics vs. porous media) causes gradient conflicts in dense neural operators.
Proposes a solution, 'Shod' (Sparse Mixture-of-Experts Routing), to manage incompatible spectral and geometric demands across different physics problems.

stories.title: Evidential Reasoning Advances Interpretable Real-World Disease Screening
stories.author: Jing Qin
bullet_points: Introduces EviScreen, a new evidential reasoning framework for disease screening.
Addresses limitations in current medical image models, specifically poor interpretability and lack of historical context.
Leverages region-level evidence from historical cases to provide transparent reasoning pathways in clinical screening.

stories.title: Text Knows What, Tables Know When: Clinical Timeline Reconstruction via Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment
stories.author: Jeremy C. Weiss
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of reconstructing precise clinical timelines from patient data.
It proposes a multimodal approach combining unstructured clinical narratives (for context) and structured EHR data (for temporal precision).
The method utilizes Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment to improve the accuracy of patient trajectory modeling, particularly for complex conditions like sepsis.

stories.title: Hand-in-the-Loop: Improving Dexterous VLA via Seamless Interventional Correction
stories.author: Ruoshi Wen
bullet_points: The research addresses compounding errors in Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models during complex, dexterous robotic manipulation.
A key challenge identified is the command mismatch when applying Interactive Imitation Learning (IIL) to high-DoF robotic hands during human takeover.
The proposed solution, 'Hand-in-the-Loop,' aims to improve dexterity by enabling seamless interventional correction.

stories.title: Self-Distilled Agentic Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Yongliang Shen
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitation of coarse, trajectory-level reward signals in Reinforcement Learning (RL) for LLM agents.
It introduces On-Policy Self-Distillation (OPSD) to provide dense, token-level guidance using a teacher branch with privileged context.
A key challenge identified is the instability when transferring OPSD to multi-turn agents due to compounding supervision issues.

stories.title: FutureSim: Replaying World Events to Evaluate Adaptive Agents
stories.author: Jonas Geiping
bullet_points: Introduces FutureSim, a new simulation framework designed to test AI agents in dynamic, real-world environments.
The core innovation is replaying real-world events chronologically, allowing agents to interact with historical data streams (like news articles).
This setup enables agents to forecast future events beyond their training knowledge cutoff while maintaining chronological context.

stories.title: Is Grep All You Need? How Agent Harnesses Reshape Agentic Search
stories.author: Vamse Kumar Subbiah
bullet_points: The paper addresses the gap in systematic comparison regarding how retrieval strategy interacts with agent architecture in agentic search.
It focuses on the complex workflows enabled by LLM agents, which autonomously retrieve information, call tools, and reason over large datasets.
The research highlights the practical need to evaluate retrieval choices beyond standard Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) implementations.

stories.title: Text Knows What, Tables Know When: Clinical Timeline Reconstruction via Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment
stories.author: Jeremy C. Weiss
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of reconstructing precise clinical timelines from patient data.
It combines unstructured clinical narratives (rich context) with structured EHR data (temporal precision).
The proposed method uses Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment to improve timeline accuracy for complex conditions like sepsis.

stories.title: MeMo: Memory as a Model
stories.author: Armando Solar-Lezama
bullet_points: Introduces MeMo (Memory as a Model), a framework to update LLMs with new, domain-specific knowledge.
MeMo achieves this by encoding new information into a dedicated memory model without altering the core LLM parameters.
Addresses the limitation of LLMs being 'frozen' after pretraining, making them more suitable for real-world, evolving applications.

stories.title: MemEye: A Visual-Centric Evaluation Framework for Multimodal Agent Memory
stories.author: Ruixiang Tang
bullet_points: Introduces MemEye, a new evaluation framework for multimodal agent memory.
Addresses a gap in current evaluations by focusing on the preservation of fine-grained visual evidence.
Targets harder reasoning cases that require tracking changes across visual states, moving beyond simple textual inference.

stories.title: Building "Sweets Vault" - a multimodal Gemini Agent with physical hardware integration
stories.author: rsamborski
bullet_points: The project involves building a multimodal Gemini Agent named "Sweets Vault."
It integrates physical hardware, suggesting a tangible application of AI.
The stated goal is to motivate young children (seven-year-olds) to complete educational tasks like reading and handwriting.

stories.title: Bigger AI models aren't always better. Here's how to actually choose.
stories.author: rohini_gaonkar
bullet_points: Challenges the assumption that larger AI models inherently perform better.
Focuses on practical guidance for users on how to select appropriate AI models.
Highlights the issue of AI model hallucination as a key consideration in model choice.
